title: kb.conflict v1
description: Emitted by kb from three distinct call sites with three distinct payload shapes, unified under one channel: (a) src/commands/conflicts.rs:287 — main `kb conflicts` detection loop, adds min_confidence and a 2-element `entries` array of {id,title,confidence,stance}; (b) src/commands/hardline.rs:203 — birth-time check when a newly-registered hardline opposes a standing one, `entries` array elements have only {id}, signal/severity hardcoded to 'hardline-polarity'/'hard'; (c) src/commands/stray.rs:114 — a proposal/action matches a registered hardline, uses hardline_id/polarity/authority/overlap/contested instead of entries. Only the fields common to all three call sites are required here; schema is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties: true) rather than a strict oneOf, since the call sites diverge structurally. Source: /kb.

project.description: Project the conflict was detected in.

min_confidence.description: conflicts.rs call site only: minimum confidence among the opposing entries.

entries.description: conflicts.rs/hardline.rs call sites only: the opposing pair. Shape varies — conflicts.rs includes {id,title,confidence,stance} per entry; hardline.rs includes only {id}.

hardline_id.description: stray.rs call site only: the registered hardline this action/proposal matched.

polarity.description: stray.rs call site only: the hardline's polarity.

authority.description: stray.rs call site only: authority backing the hardline, if set.

overlap.description: stray.rs call site only: overlapping terms/spans between the action and the hardline.

contested.description: stray.rs call site only: whether the match is contested.
